context: Tests whether the agent recommends and applies progressive disclosure principles: identifying content from SKILL.md that duplicates detail already in REFERENCE.md and recommending linking instead of inlining. The revised SKILL.md should be significantly shorter by leveraging the existing reference file.

checklist.name: Linking over inlining
checklist.description: Recommendations explicitly state that content should link to REFERENCE.md rather than being inlined in SKILL.md

checklist.name: Reference file identified
checklist.description: Recommendations specifically identify REFERENCE.md (by name) as the file to link to for detailed content

checklist.name: Severity mappings removed
checklist.description: Revised SKILL.md removes inline severity color/urgency mapping tables (they exist in REFERENCE.md) and links there instead

checklist.name: Flag tables removed
checklist.description: Revised SKILL.md removes detailed flag reference tables (these are in REFERENCE.md) — tables like the Slack/Email/PagerDuty flag lists

checklist.name: Template list removed
checklist.description: Revised SKILL.md removes the inline list of available email templates (already in REFERENCE.md) and links there instead

checklist.name: SKILL.md substantially shorter
checklist.description: Output SKILL.md is at least 40% shorter in line count than the input SKILL.md

checklist.name: Core examples preserved
checklist.description: Output SKILL.md retains the main quickstart command examples for each channel (the bash code blocks showing basic usage)

checklist.name: Before/after shown
checklist.description: recommendations.md includes before/after text for at least two of the proposed changes

checklist.name: WHY explained
checklist.description: recommendations.md explains why progressive disclosure (linking vs inlining) improves the skill — not just what to change

checklist.name: REFERENCE.md not modified
checklist.description: REFERENCE.md content is not changed or recreated — only SKILL.md is produced as modified output
